channel.title: NYT > Arts
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Stolen Letters That John Keats Sent to His Beloved Are Found
item.description: The eight letters by the 19th-century Romantic poet to his fiancée, Fanny Brawne, were taken decades ago from a Whitney family estate on Long Island.
item.dc:creator: Tom Mashberg
item.media:credit: From left: Science Source/Photo Researchers History, via Getty Images; Alamy
item.media:description: The dozens of letters that John Keats wrote to Fanny Brawne between 1819 and 1820 have long been considered literary treasures.

item.title: New York City Ballet’s Spring Season: Dancers to Watch
item.description: New York City Ballet opens its spring season with classic works by George Balanchine and more. Here are some dancers who will surely stand out.
item.dc:creator: Gia Kourlas

item.title: Singer D4vd Charged With Murder of Celeste Rivas Hernandez
item.description: The singer is accused of sexually abusing Celeste Rivas Hernandez before killing the teenager with a sharp weapon and mutilating her body. He has pleaded not guilty.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens and Emmanuel Morgan
item.media:description: The singer D4vd, 21, created the anthem for Fortnite. The severely decomposed remains of Celeste Rivas Hernandez were discovered in his car’s trunk in September.

item.title: How Coachella Became a Pop Spectacle With Surprise Guests Like Madonna and Olivia Rodrigo
item.description: What started in the late ’90s as a rock and dance-music festival has evolved into a platform where social media, spectacle and unexpected guests make the headlines.
item.dc:creator: Caryn Ganz
item.category: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival
item.media:description: Sabrina Carpenter, left, invited Madonna to perform in her headlining set during Coachella’s second weekend.

item.title: Review: Rose Byrne and Kelli O’Hara Seduce in ‘Fallen Angels’
item.description: Lust is the comic engine driving the action of a riotous revival of one of Noël Coward’s early plays, with Rose Byrne and Kelli O’Hara, at Todd Haimes Theater.
item.dc:creator: Jason Zinoman

item.title: With Orban Out, the Pianist András Schiff Plans a Return to Hungary
item.description: Schiff has said he won’t perform in countries with what he calls strongmen leaders, a list that still includes the United States and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nagourney
item.media:credit: Vincent Tullo for The New York Times
item.media:description: Andras Schiff, here at Carnegie Hall in 2019, said he was stunned and gratified by Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s defeat.

item.title: ‘Reefer Madness,’ the P.S.A. That Backfired Spectacularly
item.description: The comically self-serious and outrageous 1936 morality tale, which warned the public about marijuana, became an unintentional parody and midnight-movie classic decades later.
item.dc:creator: Maya Salam

item.title: To Play a Man With Tourette’s, He Swapped the Script for Real Life
item.description: Robert Aramayo immersed himself in John Davidson’s life to prepare for the movie “I Swear.” It earned Aramayo a BAFTA, but Davidson’s tics became the talking point.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli
item.category: British Academy of Film and Television
item.media:credit: Damien Maloney for The New York Times

item.title: With Trump Novices, Can the U.S. Win the ‘Art Olympics’?
item.description: After the State Department overhauled the process for choosing an artist for the Venice Biennale, it gave control to a woman who previously owned a pet food store.
item.dc:creator: Zachary Small

item.title: Luca Guadagnino Wants a Difficult Opera to Break Free of Polemics
item.description: The Italian filmmaker hopes his staging of John Adams’s “The Death of Klinghoffer” will help audiences look past the controversies surrounding the work.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo
item.media:credit: Clara Vannucci for The New York Times
item.media:description: “For me, what matters is that — from a musical standpoint, from the perspective of the libretto — this is a major work,” Luca Guadagnino said.

item.title: ‘Half Man,’ and 5 More Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: This new series, starring, written and directed by Richard Gadd of “Baby Reindeer,” comes to HBO, and Hulu observes 420 with a cavalcade of cannabis series.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez
item.media:description: From left: Jamie Bell and Richard Gadd in “Half Man.”

item.title: How Jason Chambers Runs a Tight Ship on ‘Below Deck’
item.description: On the current season of the Bravo reality show, he has his hands full with real housewives and more. These are the things that keep him grounded.
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Shattuck

item.title: ‘The Comeback’ Season 3, Episode 5 Recap: No Captain
item.description: “How’s That?!” needs a real leader. The guys surrounding Valerie right now clearly aren’t up to the task.
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray
item.media:description: Brandon (Andrew Scott) tells Valerie (Lisa Kudrow) that her show is his least troublesome production. That probably isn’t a good thing.

item.title: Lana Del Rey, Prince: 10 Songs We’re Talking About This Week
item.description: Lana Del Rey reveals a dramatic theme for a James Bond video game, and a look back at the chart from 40 years ago, when Prince reigned supreme.
item.dc:creator: Jon Pareles
item.media:description: Lana Del Rey’s new “First Light” provides the theme for the Bond video game “007 First Light.”

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Refers to Kash Patel’s Job in the Past Tense
item.description: The late night host called him “our soon-to-be former F.B.I. director” after publication of an article in The Atlantic alleging excessive drinking and unexplained absences.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: How an Art Event in the California Desert Became a Community
item.description: As High Desert Test Sites looks toward its 25th anniversary, its founder, and various participants, look back.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Gallow

item.title: An Artist Asks: Without Darkness, Who Are We?
item.description: After gathering ideas from entomologists, neurobiologists and others, Jan Tichy created an exhibition responding to the effects of our disappearing nights.
item.dc:creator: Zoë Lescaze
item.category: Broad, Eli and Edythe, Art Museum (Lansing, Mich)

item.title: ‘Schmigadoon!’ Review: Oh, What a Beautiful Sendup
item.description: Cinco Paul’s loving spoof of Golden Age musicals, adapted from a TV series, comes to Broadway, where its charming musical numbers can really shine.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli and Sara Krulwich

item.title: An L.A. Artist Devoted to the Process of Paint
item.description: Sandy Rodriguez is a painter but also, on some level, a scientist, an art historian, a botanist, an outdoorswoman, and maybe even an alchemist.
item.dc:creator: Ted Loos
item.media:credit: Stella Kalinina for The New York Times
item.media:description: “I make all my own paint,” said Sandy Rodriguez, 50, who can talk for hours about her process, and, more important to her, the history and meaning of all her choices.

item.title: The Next Frontier in Design? The Humble Birdhouse.
item.description: Recently, as artists and architects have turned their attention outdoors, they have started putting their creative chops to work — for the avians among us.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Perry
item.media:credit: Kirsten Luce for The New York Times

item.title: Patrick Muldoon, Actor on ‘Days of Our Lives’ and ‘Melrose Place,’ Dies at 57
item.description: He played the handsome Austin Reed on the NBC daytime soap opera “Days of Our Lives” in more than 400 episodes.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz
item.media:description: Patrick Muldoon in 2024 at the Toronto International Film Festival.

item.title: Could ‘A River Runs Through It’ Have Been a Hit Today?
item.description: The autobiographical novella, first published 50 years ago, arguably created a new type of guy: the literary fly fisherman.
item.dc:creator: Monte Burke

item.title: Met Gala 2026 Guide: Theme, Hosts, Dress Code and More
item.description: What to know about this year’s celebration, including the return of a superstar last seen on the Met carpet 10 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:description: Beyoncé arriving to the Met Gala in 2016, the last year she attended the event.

item.title: U.S. Museums Reach Deep into America’s Past
item.description: Museums around the country are celebrating the nation’s heritage in ways that go beyond what might be considered traditional.
item.dc:creator: Alina Tugend
item.category: Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art

item.title: Gwendolyn Chisolm, Who Rhymed on Rap’s First Female Hit, Dies at 66
item.description: A chance encounter led to overnight success for the Sequence, a seminal trio whose “Funk You Up” broke barriers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Williams
item.media:description: The Sequence in 1980. From left: Cheryl Cook (Cheryl the Pearl), Gwendolyn Chisolm (Blondy) and Angela Brown, later Angie Stone (Angie B.).

item.title: Group Efforts Succeed During Milan Design Week
item.description: Collaborations among designers allow for new creations during the event.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: At Milan Design Week, Function and Form Take on New Meanings
item.description: From a spinning Christmas tree to tables that resemble flowers are among the pieces on display this week.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Vintage Designs Take on New Lives at Milan Design Week
item.description: Revived pieces, including a 1950s table by Carlo Mollino and a 1960s lamp by Carlo Nason, are on display at the annual festival of furnishings.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: At Milan Design Week, Creative Seating Brings Fresh Ideas to the Table
item.description: A crisp couch, a monolithic chair and a playful pouf highlight the ways designers are reimagining how we recline.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: A Fountain of Creativity from Arid Uzbekistan
item.description: A design exhibition inspired by the Central Asian republic’s cultural traditions makes its debut in the heart of Milan.
item.dc:creator: Ifeoluwa Adedeji

item.title: How Two Men with Hard Heads Broke Through Murano’s Glass Ceiling
item.description: The founders of the glass brand 6:AM bring a punk attitude to the production of dazzling pieces.
item.dc:creator: Laura May Todd
item.media:credit: Matteo de Mayda for The New York Times
item.media:description: Edoardo Pandolfo, left, and Francesco Palù, founders of the glass brand 6:AM. The two create custom Murano glass installations, while producing their own furniture, lighting and objects.

item.title: A Time of Growth for Museums for Children
item.description: Across the nation, news museums are opening, and existing ones are expanding.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora
item.category: Kansas Children's Discovery Center (Topeka, Kan, Museum)

item.title: A New Museum in California Aims to Draw Children to Science
item.description: The $7 million KidSTREAM museum was the brainchild of a former teacher looking for a place to entertain her young daughters.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora
item.media:credit: Adam Amengual for The New York Times
item.media:description: A whales at play sculpture at the KidSTREAM museum in Camarillo, Calif.

item.title: Marshawn Lynch on ‘Euphoria’ and Not Disappearing Into His Roles
item.description: “You know what you’re going to get with me,” said Lynch, who went from candid athlete to coveted actor in “Euphoria” and more.
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Morgan
item.media:description: “He has this natural charisma,” another “Euphoria” actor said of Marshawn Lynch. “It’s like an innate confidence, a sense of self, and he oozes that.”

item.title: Wisconsin’s Chazen Museum of Art Explores New Ways to Display Its Collection
item.description: At the Chazen Museum of Art in Wisconsin, each gallery will now have a single “focus object,” with “a constellation of other artworks” helping to draw out particular themes.
item.dc:creator: Ted Loos

item.title: After the Heists: Securing Museums Without Closing Them Off
item.description: The bold robbery at the Louvre shocked the world, but all museums face the challenge of protecting art and historical treasures.
item.dc:creator: Sam Lubell
item.category: Chihuly Garden and Glass (Seattle, Wash, Museum)
item.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
item.media:description: A variety of new walls step along the sloping site of the Norton Simon Museum, including a textured white concrete barrier in various segments, to the east and a matte white wall to the west.

item.title: A Dance Party for Martha Graham With Ariana DeBose and Padma Lakshmi
item.description: Stars and luminaries honored the dance company’s 100th anniversary with dinner, performances and a champagne toast at the New York Public Library.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr and Rebecca Smeyne
item.category: Graham, Martha, Center of Contemporary Dance
New York Public Library for the Performing Arts
item.media:credit: Rebecca Smeyne for The New York Times

item.title: Paul Waldman, Creator and Curator of Art Museum for Birds, Dies at 89
item.description: A dyslexic teenager, he reinvented himself as a bodybuilder. Then he turned to art, producing transgressive paintings and elaborate birdhouses.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Rosenwald
item.media:credit: Michael Shavel for The New York Times
item.media:description: Paul Waldman in 1993 with the International Bird Museum that he built on the roof of his house in Southampton, N.Y. The multistory birdhouse had an art gallery that displayed miniature works by Roy Lichtenstein and others.

item.title: A Chicago Man’s 10,000 Concerts on Tape Are Becoming Digital History
item.description: From the mid-1980s until just a few years ago, Aadam Jacobs recorded thousands of club shows, including an early Nirvana performance. Volunteers are adding them to an online archive.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb
item.media:description: Aadam Jacobs at home in Chicago. The Chicago Tribune called him, among other things, “a tireless audio documentarian” for recording thousands of intimate concerts in his hometown.

item.title: At Milan Design Week, Noodling Around With an Italian Classic
item.description: An exhibition of 3-D-printed pasta reveals what could be the most disruptive culinary innovation since the eggbeater.
item.dc:creator: Ian Volner

item.title: Anne Hathaway and Michaela Coel in a Spooky, Tangled Thriller
item.description: Our film critic Alissa Wilkinson reviews “Mother Mary.”
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson and Edward Vega

item.title: Barber Osgerby, a British Design Team, Is Honored in Milan
item.description: The Triennale design museum is hosting an exhibition of work by the renowned studio Barber Osgerby.
item.dc:creator: Bridget Goldberg

item.title: Lawsuit Against Alec Baldwin for ‘Rust’ Shooting Heads Toward Trial
item.description: Although the criminal case against Mr. Baldwin was thrown out, the actor still faces civil cases about the fatal shooting of a cinematographer in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs
item.media:description: A judge dismissed a criminal case against Alec Baldwin in 2024 after determining that the prosecution had withheld evidence.

item.title: The Politically Charged Art of Celeste Dupuy-Spencer
item.description: Her vivid paintings examined religious fundamentalism and events like the Jan. 6 riots at the U.S. Capitol. She died this month at 46.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Celeste Dupuy-Spencer, Painter of Hotly Topical Images, Dies at 46
item.description: Her vibrant yet brooding work explored the crises of the Trump era, with paintings depicting the Jan. 6 riots and a mangled Confederate monument.
item.dc:creator: Alex Williams

item.title: An Explosion Rattles a Toronto Neighborhood. A Drake Video Was to Blame.
item.description: A blast on Thursday night turned out to be for a video shoot, but it unnerved residents who had lived through a propane plant explosion in 2008.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:description: Drake performing in London last year. He posted two images on social media of an explosion that startled residents near Downsview Park in Toronto.

item.title: Kanye West’s Concert in Poland Is Canceled Amid Furor Over Antisemitic Comments
item.description: The announcement came a day after a government official said, “In a country marked by the history of the Holocaust, we cannot pretend that this is just entertainment.”
item.dc:creator: Michaela Towfighi
item.media:description: After Ye performed two nights in Los Angeles on a comeback tour, he has had concerts across Europe canceled.
